Robe of Enshroudment  
 

Lore Item
Slot: CHEST
AC: 9
INT: +4 AGI: +4
SV FIRE: +4 SV COLD: +4
Focus: Reanimation Haste II
WT: 3.5 Size: MEDIUM
Class: NEC
Race: HUM ERU DEF GNM IKS FRG DRK
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Slot 2, Type 21 (Special Ornamentation)

AC: 9
INT: +4 AGI: +4
SV FIRE: +4 SV COLD: +4

Appearance:Plain Robe
Tint:
 
Color (RGB):1, 1, 1
Stackable:No

Page Updated:Tue Dec 20th, 2022

Expansion: Original
Average Price: 367pp Pricing Data...
Rarity: Common
Level to Attain: 28

This item is the result of a quest.

Ok people... Innoruuk has been flodded in the last few days with low lvl Necros wearing the robe... Definatly a new vendor item... Check my other post for details...

Got it cheep!
QuoteReply
# Nov 22 2002 at 7:14 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1593362014850
View User Forum
68 posts
Yep I'm one of those who buy items and give them to My new Charactors. In less than 1 year I have 8 Charactors and all of them are very well equipted. They all do their part by supplying loot which My Druid picks up and delivers to My trader. Even My trader makes loads of Plat by buying cheep and reselling.
What the He(( is wrong with that?
Got this for 75p in the Bazaar last night, Man does it look GREAT on MY level 1 gnome Necro!!!!
Got a pair of Spider fur Gloves for 100p too!!!
I think peeps who complain about Twinking don't know how to do it themselves.

The picture of the male DE in the fashion plate for this item is wearing purple armor, not a black robe. I tried to E-mail Alakhazam but I dont have a hotmail account. Just to let you know it probably is the wrong pic.
▲ Page top
Matter of taste
QuoteReply
# Mar 08 2002 at 1:04 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591750384971
7 posts
Learn how game works for one,I wear this robe still at 52,why?I did quest around 30,and I love the look with a few items you hit the soft cap of 200 int.Thats all you need.If u plan on getting hit alot after say 30 well your not a very savy player,much less necro.I'll never change this robe cause I dont need to,soloed my whole career.(and have 8, 32 plus characters of which necro is main).I love the fact that kids distain it,now it's my trademark as I envisioned.
Yorga Dablacula 52 nec et all
▲ Page top
RE: Matter of taste
QuoteReply
# Aug 18 2002 at 4:51 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1593966118024
319 posts
Yes this robe looks awesome, and I would put it on my necro in a heartbeat. Sure thae stats arent the best, but until you can get somethin better, why not have fun and look decent?

▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Apr 04 2002 at 2:40 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) 255 is the soft cap now man, get a new robe
#REDACTED, Posted: May 13 2002 at 6:13 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) ummm no it isnt.
Int/Wis cap...
QuoteReply
# May 15 2002 at 1:15 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592253816602
41 posts
Actually, 200 is still the cap, but you do get more mana from int/wis after 200 than you used to. so getting to 255 int/wis *does* have more of a point now than it used to. However, for Necromancers I'd suggest dropping the quest for int gear after 200int and going for HPs. Why? your hp - Mana conversion spell. The more hps you got = the more mana you got.
▲ Page top
RE: Int/Wis cap...
QuoteReply
# Nov 26 2002 at 1:37 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592759911393
61 posts
/agrees. ya, necros have little need for uber int, they make their mana as they go, much better to invest in resist, ac, and hp gear for a necro once you get around 200 int. regardless of what anyone says, if you solo with a caster, your eventually going to get beat on a little and when you drain your hp for mana, a twinked out hp and ac just in case for protection makes much more sence than alot of useless int that you can boast about at your bind point while waiting for that rez you'll be needing.
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Feb 13 2002 at 12:46 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Why would anyone waste their time either doing the quest or getting someone else to kill the sisters, This robe can't hold a candle to my bone casters robe.

QuoteReply
# Feb 23 2002 at 4:49 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1594570537980

Good Deal
QuoteReply
# Sep 01 2001 at 6:40 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1593361547458
1 post
Talk about all the LUCK!! My roomate was wondering around Neriak-Foreign Quarter, when he stumbles upon a merchant. A merchant who so happens to have a Robe of Enshroudment. And guess what he paid for it. 44pp. I dunno if the price changed or not on the Tunare server. But damn man, thats cheap!

PS: This is still to be uncovered as truth. I find it hard to believe he could of just found it on some NPC in Neriak, but then again who knows =/

# Feb 23 2002 at 6:58 PM Rating: Default

LOL you should see some of the stuff I sell to merchants. I was cleaning out my bank the other day and sold almost a whole set of Jaundice bone and DW armor to the merchant in Rivervale bank.

RE: Good Deal
QuoteReply
# Sep 04 2001 at 11:27 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591765385143
48 posts
If a merchant has room for an Item when you sell to him, he will offer that Item up for sale at whatever he thinks is fair. Granted what NPC merchants think as fair is a little silly. (um 2157pp for an Axe of the Iron Back or 836pp for a lamentation).
▲ Page top
RE: Good Deal
QuoteReply

Lets argue over a junk robe
QuoteReply
# May 10 2001 at 5:45 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592245901759
48 posts
So what.. it comes from lev 30's via a quest.. and for higher levels by killing the three sisters.. who cares.. do me a favor.. and do not rate people's posts just cuz they found the item via a different method. This robe is Oh.. a low end twink item now.. sure it rocked when it was found.. but now newbies run 'round in oracle robes because those are bout 500pp on my server..
so who cares if somebody mentions it comes from a KILL.. they can have some high lev friends go in an kill the sisters in a matter of minutes instead of doing the quest...

BE HAPPY THIS ISNT ONE OF INNORUUKS NEW DROPS :P
▲ Page top
RE: Lets argue over a junk robe
QuoteReply
# Jul 08 2001 at 4:52 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592249820303
14 posts
I completely agree with this. Do the quest for yourself like the game is meant to be. Dont have all the high lvlers waste the sisters for you. I remeber the days when EQ came out and people had to do all the discovery on their own. Those were the days when a Robe of Enshourdment was a token of respect from the other players. Now lvl two twinks and noobies who are hooked on the game by friends run around with these +4 intelligence robes, really irks me. O well, such is the way of the EQ gods to grant the requests of the people who exploit all that is good and sacred about this game.
▲ Page top
Zaarkal
QuoteReply
# Mar 20 2001 at 7:42 AM Rating: Decent
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
I looted one of these at lev 3 from a party killing the sisters in Neriak. You can get it there...

The preferred method would be the quest, since it does not destroy your faction.

From Neriek

# Mar 08 2001 at 1:26 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
U can also get this from Neriek Forien Quater by killing the X'ta Sisters. I had a lvl 52 Necro kill one and let me loot. U will lose faction with Neriek though.
